repository_ssim: St. Catherine's Monastery of the Sinai
contents_tesim: The folios contain an unknown kanon on Saint Nicholas. It is not clear whether the folios belonged to a Menaion, an Oktoechos, or a manuscript intended for private devotion (Rossetto)
full_text_tesim: A single folio of Sin. gr. 962 corresponds to a single folio of the original manuscript, and hence the original manuscript was probably of similar dimensions: 150x100 mm. The Schriftspiegel is approximately 110x70 mm, with an outer margin of 25 mm and a lower of 20 mm
Each side of the bifolio preserves 18 to 20 lines of text, 6 mm apart and arranged in one column
The folios contain an unknown kanon on Saint Nicholas. It is not clear whether the folios belonged to a Menaion, an Oktoechos, or a manuscript intended for private devotion (Rossetto)
The writing runs parallel to the overtext, rotated through 180°
Upright, round, calligraphic minuscule with thick, pronounced strokes. The writing is pendent from the ruled lines. Each ode begins with an initial in epigraphischer Auszeichnungsmajuskel
